continuityTheme: AI approval packets move quickly, but the final owner-of-record still changes too often between policy, security, and product.
boardQuestion: Which AI lane needs a locked owner path before the board trusts another approval cycle?
continuityHeadline: AI owner continuity is thinner than the current board packet implies.
continuitySignal: The final owner changes across policy, security, and product more often than the board packet acknowledges.
headline: AI lanes need a locked owner path before the next board packet expands scope.
nextMove: Lock one AI owner path and require owner-change logging for any override.

continuityTheme: Identity exceptions close slowly because owner changes are not being written back into one stable continuity path.
boardQuestion: Which identity lane needs less owner drift before another exception cycle starts?
continuityHeadline: Identity owner continuity drifts faster than the board packet explains.
continuitySignal: Final calls are being made, but the owner path is incomplete after audit-driven overrides.
headline: Identity needs a cleaner owner continuity trail before the next audit packet lands.
nextMove: Reduce owner drift in identity before the next exception window opens.

continuityTheme: Revenue packets reopen because the handoff chain is recorded, but continuity breaks are never repaired into one stable owner path.
boardQuestion: Which reporting lane needs its owner continuity repaired before another forecast packet loops back to the board?
continuityHeadline: Revenue owner continuity breaks are staying open across repeated forecast cycles.
continuitySignal: The board packet shows who reviewed the decision, but not which owner still carries it after the last reopen.
headline: Revenue packets need open continuity breaks repaired before another cycle starts.
nextMove: Repair the revenue owner path across finance and commercial signoff before the next forecast review.

continuityTheme: FinTech scope looks active, but the final owner chain is unstable because too many reviews shift ownership between decisions.
boardQuestion: Which capital-sensitive lane needs a more stable owner chain before it can add more scope?
continuityHeadline: FinTech owner continuity is now a scope-quality problem, not just a process problem.
continuitySignal: Merchant, treasury, and KYC reviews keep shifting the final owner path without a stable chain.
headline: FinTech owner continuity is too unstable for another scope expansion.
nextMove: Stabilize the FinTech owner chain and freeze ad hoc owner shifts until the continuity path holds.

continuityTheme: Procurement remains the cleanest owner-continuity lane and can serve as the reference path for the rest of the estate.
boardQuestion: Which lane already shows stable owner continuity and can be copied elsewhere?
continuityHeadline: Procurement already behaves like the owner-continuity reference model.
continuitySignal: Trust and commercial packets close with a consistent owner-of-record and low continuity drift.
headline: Procurement shows the continuity pattern the rest of the estate should copy.
nextMove: Reuse the procurement owner path in one constrained adjacent lane.

continuityTheme: Regulated control lanes still depend on one-off owner resets instead of a durable continuity scorecard.
boardQuestion: Which regulated lane needs a stronger owner-continuity path before the board scales another control family?
continuityHeadline: Biotech needs stronger owner continuity across regulated decisions.
continuitySignal: CAPA, specimen, and safety packets still show too many owner shifts between reviews.
headline: Biotech needs a durable owner path before more control-family expansion.
nextMove: Lock the biotech owner path and review every regulated owner shift before the next expansion.
